title: Brainstorming CoPilot
summary: Explore how a conversational chatbot supports idea generation with text-based prompts, sample outputs, and reusable example starters.
inputPlaceholder: Paste the brainstorming prompt here or start from one of the examples below.
outputCaption: The response panel shows a sample text output defined in the probe data for this page.
examplesCaption: These prompts are meant to be copied into the input box so participants can see a representative brainstorming exchange.

examplePrompts.label: Teacher PD ideas
examplePrompts.prompt: Brainstorm workshop ideas for helping teachers learn to use generative AI responsibly in their lesson planning. I want options that are practical, collaborative, and easy to pilot.
examplePrompts.output:
Here are several workshop directions to explore.

1. Prompt makeover lab
Teachers bring a real planning task and iteratively improve prompts together.

2. Risk spotting clinic
Participants review AI-generated lesson materials and identify accuracy, bias, and accessibility concerns.

3. Before-and-after planning sprint
Teachers compare a lesson planned without AI and one planned with AI support, then discuss what changed.

4. Subject-specific design tables
Break into groups by subject area so teachers can generate examples that feel immediately usable.

5. Reflection wall
End with a shared discussion on where AI saved time, where it created new work, and where teacher judgment mattered most.

examplePrompts.label: Student support concepts
examplePrompts.prompt: Give me ideas for an AI-powered student support tool for first-generation college students. Focus on helpful, realistic features instead of futuristic ones.
examplePrompts.output:
Possible concept directions

- Deadline translator: turns course syllabi into a simple weekly checklist.
- Office-hours coach: helps students draft questions before meeting instructors.
- Campus process explainer: breaks down forms, advising steps, and registration tasks in plain language.
- Study plan builder: converts a goal like 'prepare for midterms' into a short daily plan.
- Reflection companion: prompts students to log wins, blockers, and support needs each week.

A strong next step would be to group these ideas into academic, administrative, and confidence-building supports.

highlights.label: Best for
highlights.value: Divergent ideation, concept expansion, and early-stage exploration
